prose:
The fukui-reactivity indices for aniline were computed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier `standard`, density-fit by default in PySCF). The calculation was performed in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was sourced from the provided input file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`, and the calculation was a single point on this fixed geometry.

The most electrophilic site is the Carbon atom (C) at 1-based index 3, with an f+ value of 2.29759. The most nucleophilic site is the Nitrogen atom (N) at 1-based index 1, with an f- value of 0.20452.

The calculated fukui indices for each atom are as follows (atom symbol, 0-based index, f+, f-, f0, dual descriptor):
